name: Old-Fashioned Cinnamon Swirl Loaf
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup butter or margarine, cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
In a large bowl, combine 2 cups flour, 1/3 cup sugar, undissolved yeast, and salt. Heat milk, water, and 1/3 cup butter until very warm (120°F to 130°F); stir into dry ingredients. Stir in eggs and enough remaining flour to make soft dough. Knead on lightly floured surface until smooth and elastic, about 6 to 8 minutes. Cover; let rest on floured surface 10 minutes.
To fill and shape loaves: Divide dough in half. Roll each half to a 16-x-7-inch rectangle. Brush each rectangle with 3 tablespoons melted butter to within 1 inch of edges. Combine 1/4 cup sugar, cinnamon, and raisins; sprinkle evenly over dough. Beginning at the short end of each rectangle, roll up tightly as for jelly roll; pinch seams and ends to seal. Place, seam sides down, in 2 greased 9-x-5-inch loaf pans. Cover; let rise in warm place until doubled, about 30 to 45 minutes. Brush loaves with egg white; sprinkle with Crumb Topping. Bake at 375°F for 40 to 45 minutes or until done. Remove from pans; let cool on wire racks.
Crumb Topping:
In a small bowl, combine 1/2 cup all-purpose flour, 1/3 cup firmly packed light brown sugar, and 1 teaspoon Ground Cinnamon. Cut in 3 tablespoons cold butter or margarine until mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
